ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRIAN MARKHAM'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART. MOTIONS FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE PLEADINGS ARE DIRECTED TO THE SOUND DISCRETION OF THE JUDGE. [CCP 473(A)(1); AND SEE CCP 576] THE COURT HAS DISCRETION TO DENY LEAVE TO AMEND WHERE A PROPOSED AMENDMENT FAILS TO STATE A VALID CAUSE OF ACTION OR DEFENSE. [SEE CALIFORNIA CASUALTY GENERAL INS. CO. V. SUP.CT. (1985) 173 CA3D 274, 280-281] HERE, PLAINTIFF STATES IN HIS MOVING PAPERS THAT HE REQUESTS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT FOR THREE REASONS: (1) TO SUBMIT A WRITTEN COMPLAINT IN PLACE OF A FORM COMPLAINT; (2) TO ELIMINATE THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY; AND (3) TO ELIMINATE FROM THE COMPLAINT DEFENDANTS WHO HAVE EITHER SETTLED OR HAVE BEEN DISMISSED. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT IN THE ABOVE PROPOSED MANNER. HOWEVER, P'S MOTION TO ADD THE LANGUAGE IN PROPOSED MOTION 27 IS DENIED. P HAS NOT REQUESTED HE BE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD ANY NEW CLAIMS IN THE FAC, HE HAS NOT DISCUSSED ADDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN HIS MOVING PAPERS, AND THE PROPOSED FAC FAILS TO STATE A VALID CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. IN THE ORIGINAL FORM COMPLAINT PLAINTIFF DID NOT CHECK THE BOX FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, AND HE HAS NOT REQUESTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THUS, PLAINTIFF IS NOT GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THEREFORE, ANY LANGUAGE IN THE PROPOSED FAC ALLEGING PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/SB

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SEGWAY SF BAY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION as to Plaintiff Brian Markham's claims for strict liability and breach of implied warranty is granted. Defendant satisfied its initial burden and plaintiff has not presented any facts establishing a triable issue as to either claim. Strict liability is inapplicable to a party such as defendant who is outside the stream of commerce for the product for which strict liability is asserted. While plaintiff no longer maintains a claims for breach of implied warranty and thus did not oppose the motion as to that claim, defendant is entitled to summary adjudication as to that claim. Off Calendar Aug-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CYNTHIA PHAM OF THE RALLO LAW FIRM, P.C., TEL. NO. 1-714-850-0690 EXT. 239 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. STEPHEN DAHM OF THE CESARI, WERNER & MORIARTY, TEL. NO. 1-650-991-5126 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BRIAN MARKHAM's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is Granted in part. Motions for leave to amend the pleadings are directed to the sound discretion of the judge. [CCP 473(a)(1); and see CCP 576] The Court has discretion to deny leave to amend where a proposed amendment fails to state a valid cause of action or defense. [See California Casualty General Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1985) 173 CA3d 274, 280-281] Here, Plaintiff states in his moving papers that he requests leave to amend the complaint for three reasons: (1) to submit a written complaint in place of a form Complaint; (2) to eliminate the cause of action for Premises Liability; and (3) to eliminate from the Complaint defendants who have either settled or have been dismissed. The Court grants Plaintiff to amend the Complaint in the above proposed manner. However, P's motion to add the language in proposed motion 27 is denied. P has not requested he be granted leave to amend to add any new claims in the FAC, he has not discussed adding punitive damages in his moving papers, and the proposed FAC fails to state a valid cause of action for punitive damages. In the original Form Complaint Plaintiff did not check the box for punitive damages, and he has not requested leave to amend to add punitive damages. Thus, Plaintiff is not granted leave to amend to add punitive damages. Therefore, any language in the proposed FAC alleging punitive damages is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
TIN WESTEN, ESQ., 714.850.0690 X239, FOR PLAINTIFF BRIAN MARKHAM.
STEFANI SALT, ESQ., 415.391.7111, FOR DEFENDANT SEGWAY SF BAY.
BRIAN CEBALLO, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff did not timely comply with the claims presentation requirement of the Government Claims Act. Gov. Code sec. 911.2 requires that a claim relating to a personal injury cause of action be presented no later than six months after the accrual of the cause of action. Plaintiff's cause of action accrued on August 17, 2012. Plaintiff did not file his claim until July 12, 2013. Plaintiff did not timely apply for leave to file a late claim. Plaintiff's delayed discovery argument is rejected. The complaint pleads Plaintiff was injured on August 17, 2012. "So long as a suspicion exists, it is clear that the plaintiff must go find the facts; she cannot wait for the facts to find her." (Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1110.) Further, "aggrieved parties generally need not know the exact manner in which their injuries were effected, nor the identities of all parties who may have played a role therein" for a cause of action to accrue. (Bernson v. Browning-Ferris Indus. of Calif., Inc. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 926, 932.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.
